articles.title: Very heavy rainfall, hailstorm and heatwave warning issued for several states, list released | To read the entire bulletin issued by IMD | Inshorts - Inshorts
articles.full_text: The IMD has issued rainfall, hailstorm and heatwave warning for several states/UTs for Thursday. Heavy to very heavy rainfall is likely over Assam, Meghalaya, West Bengal, Sikkim, Arunachal Pradesh, Bihar, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram, Tripura, Tamil Nadu and Puducherry. Hailstorm is likely over Himachal Pradesh, J&K, Ladakh and Uttarakhand. Heatwave is likely over Maharashtra, Telangana and UP.

articles.title: India Weather Alert: Heatwave Tightens Grip While Monsoon Triggers Heavy Rain - Dailyhunt
articles.full_text:
India is witnessing two completely different weather conditions on June 18, 2026. While Uttar Pradesh, Delhi, Vidarbha and several central regions continue to battle intense heatwave conditions with temperatures touching 43°C, heavy monsoon rains are affecting eastern, northeastern and southern states.
The India Meteorological Department has issued alerts for both extreme heat and heavy rainfall as weather systems remain highly active across the country.
Where Is The Heat Hitting Hardest?
A large part of northern and central India continues to remain under severe heat stress. Dry northwesterly winds are pushing temperatures higher, making daytime conditions uncomfortable for millions.
Uttar Pradesh remains among the worst affected states. Many districts are recording temperatures between 40°C and 43°C. The combination of strong sunlight and dry winds is making outdoor activities difficult during afternoon hours.
The heat is not limited to Uttar Pradesh alone. Delhi, parts of Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh and Vidarbha are also experiencing very high temperatures. In some places, the actual feel of the temperature is even higher than what thermometers show.
Why Uttar Pradesh Is Facing Extreme Heat
Uttar Pradesh is currently under the influence of dry weather conditions. Strong northwesterly winds continue to dominate the atmosphere, preventing cloud formation and rainfall.
Cities across eastern and central Uttar Pradesh are seeing some of the hottest conditions. Areas such as Prayagraj, Mirzapur, Banda and Ghazipur remain particularly vulnerable.
In Lucknow, daytime temperatures are hovering between 38°C and 41°C. During peak afternoon hours, the heat feels much stronger. Night temperatures are also staying high, giving little relief to residents.
According to weather forecasts, heatwave conditions may continue for the next few days before significant changes arrive.
What Is Happening In Delhi And Northwest India?
Delhi and the National Capital Region are also dealing with hot and humid conditions. Temperatures are touching 40°C to 42°C in many areas.
However, weather experts are watching a fresh Western Disturbance moving into northwest India. This system may bring isolated thunderstorms, dust storms and gusty winds to parts of Delhi, Punjab, Haryana and Rajasthan later in the week.
The Western Himalayan region, including Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Jammu and Kashmir and Ladakh, is already receiving scattered rainfall. Thunderstorms and lightning activity are being reported in several locations.
Monsoon Brings Relief In Eastern India
While northern India struggles with heat, eastern India is seeing active monsoon conditions.
West Bengal and Sikkim are among the wettest regions in the country right now. Heavy to very heavy rainfall is affecting many districts. Rivers, streams and low-lying areas are being closely monitored as rainfall continues.
Bihar has also started receiving widespread monsoon showers. Rainfall activity is increasing steadily, bringing much-needed relief from earlier hot weather conditions.
Thunderstorms accompanied by strong winds are expected in several eastern districts. Residents are being advised to stay alert during periods of intense weather activity.
Northeast India Sees Continuous Rain
The monsoon remains fully active across Northeast India.
States such as Assam, Meghalaya, Arunachal Pradesh and Tripura are receiving widespread rainfall. In some locations, isolated heavy rain events may lead to temporary waterlogging and local disruptions.
Weather experts say the rain pattern is likely to continue as moisture-rich winds remain active over the region.
For farmers and water reservoirs, the rainfall is largely beneficial. However, caution remains necessary in vulnerable areas prone to flooding.
Southern States Under Monsoon Influence
The monsoon has covered most of southern India. Kerala, Karnataka and parts of Tamil Nadu are receiving regular rainfall.
Coastal Karnataka and Kerala continue to witness widespread showers. The weather remains pleasant compared to the extreme conditions seen in northern India.
Tamil Nadu and Coastal Andhra Pradesh are also seeing scattered rainfall accompanied by occasional thunderstorms.
In Telangana, some districts are still facing heatwave conditions. However, forecasts suggest thunderstorms may gradually reduce temperatures in the coming days.
Why India's Weather Looks So Different Right Now
India's current weather pattern is being shaped by multiple systems at the same time.
The Southwest Monsoon is advancing across eastern, northeastern and southern regions. At the same time, dry continental winds continue affecting northern and central India.
A fresh Western Disturbance is also influencing weather conditions in the northwest. Because of these overlapping systems, one part of the country is receiving heavy rainfall while another continues to face extreme heat.
This contrast is quite common during the transition phase of the monsoon season.
Important Safety Advice
People living in heatwave-affected regions should drink plenty of water and avoid direct sunlight during afternoon hours. Outdoor work should be reduced between noon and late afternoon whenever possible.
Those living in rainfall-affected states should remain cautious during thunderstorms. Weather officials advise avoiding open fields and large trees during lightning activity.
Keeping track of local weather bulletins can help people stay prepared for sudden changes.
What To Expect Next
Weather experts expect the monsoon to continue advancing gradually across more parts of the country. Rainfall activity may increase in several eastern and northern regions over the coming days.
At the same time, heatwave conditions are likely to persist in parts of Uttar Pradesh, Delhi, Vidarbha and central India before temperatures begin to ease.
India's weather story on June 18 is one of sharp contrasts. One region is searching for rain while another is dealing with heavy downpours. As the monsoon continues its journey, weather conditions are expected to keep changing rapidly across the nation.

articles.title: Video: ऊना में झमाझम बारिश, गर्मी से मिली राहत - Amar Ujala
articles.search_term: लू (Bilaspur OR Chamba OR Hamirpur OR Kangra OR Kinnaur OR Kullu OR "Lahaul and Spiti" OR Mandi OR Shimla OR "Sirmaur (Sirmour)" OR Solan OR Una) Himachal Pradesh
articles.full_text: ऊना जिले में गुरुवार दोपहर अचानक मौसम ने करवट ली और बारिश होने से लोगों को भीषण गर्मी व उमस से राहत मिली। सुबह से तेज धूप के कारण तापमान बढ़ा हुआ था, लेकिन दोपहर बाद आसमान में घने बादल छा गए और कई क्षेत्रों में हल्की बूंदाबांदी शुरू हो गई। बारिश के चलते मौसम सुहावना हो गया तथा तापमान में भी गिरावट दर्ज की गई। लोगों ने राहत की सांस ली और बाजारों में भी गर्मी का असर कम देखने को मिला। किसानों ने भी इस बारिश को फसलों के लिए लाभदायक बताया है।
